title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
prompt:
Given an integer array nums, return the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.

A subsequence is a sequence that can be derived from an array by deleting some or no elements without changing the order of the remaining elements.

Example: nums = [10,9,2,5,3,7,101,18] → 4 because the longest increasing subsequence is [2,3,7,101] (or [2,5,7,101], etc.).

notesForAI.coreIdea: O(n^2) DP: dp[i] = length of LIS ending at index i. For each i, check all j < i where nums[j] < nums[i]. O(n log n) optimization: maintain a 'tails' array where tails[k] is the smallest tail element of all increasing subsequences of length k+1. Use binary search to update it.
commonBugs: Using non-strict inequality (>= instead of >) allowing equal elements in the subsequence
In the O(n log n) approach, using upper_bound instead of lower_bound (or vice versa)
Returning dp array length instead of max(dp)
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(n log n) with binary search optimization, O(n^2) with basic DP
